title: Cross-repo self-heal: route harness-level observer findings to the engine repo as a triage-gated system-repair issue
description:
## Problem

When the harness runs cross-repo (a downstream user with their own fork building
their own target repo), a **harness-level** failure has nowhere to go. The
observer already classifies harness-level vs ticket-level findings and posts the
diagnosis to the run's source issue / phone (`_observe_and_report` /
`_post_harness_observation` in `adw/workflow_runner.py`), but that report lands in
the **downstream target repo** — the engine owner never sees it. So engine bugs
found by a second user (exactly the bugs self-hosting can't surface) don't flow
back upstream.

## Approach (decided)

Route the observer's harness-level finding to the **engine repo's issue tracker**,
as a **system-repair issue** (a report) — not a PR. At detection time the agent is
mid-run on a *target* ticket and has not (and may not) touched engine code, so
there is no fix to PR; the upstream harness dogfoods the fix on its own side once
the issue is triaged in. This reuses the observer + `adw/github.py` issue-filing
that already exist; it is not a new subsystem or a hook.

Trigger: observer result is harness-level **AND** the run is cross-repo
(`paths.target_root() != paths.engine_root()`). A self-hosted run (target ==
engine) keeps today's behavior exactly.
acceptance_criteria: On a harness-level observer finding during a **cross-repo** run, a
The engine repo slug is configurable (e.g. `ADW_ENGINE_REPO` /
**Human-label-gated:** the filed issue does NOT carry the `adw` label (so it is
**Deduped:** before filing, existing open issues are checked by a fingerprint
**Best-effort, never fatal:** any failure (missing token scope on the engine
A self-hosted run (target == engine) is unchanged: no upstream issue is filed.
Unit tests with a fake GitHub client cover: a cross-repo harness-level finding
Full suite stays green (`uv run pytest -q`).
